problem: Next work should be chosen by expected impact on the whole-game conformance score, not only by whichever local metric was most recently inspected.
strategy: Rank candidates by weighted overall-score lift, current gap, confidence, reuse, risk, and measured compute history.
successMeasure: Before long compute cycles, the selected work should have an explicit investment score and a measurable target/guardrail profile.

topCandidate.label: Tighten audio reference segmentation and cue matching
topCandidate.rationale: Audio is still the weakest quality category. Semantic measurement debt is reduced, and the Challenge Perfect candidate loop now shows no safe keeper yet; measured-best thin-bright-square-quiet-held should guide the next generator pass.
topCandidate.nextAction: Continue Challenge Perfect candidate generation around thin-bright-square-quiet-held; the latest sweep found no safe keeper, so do not promote a runtime cue yet.

candidates.label: Tighten audio reference segmentation and cue matching
candidates.rationale: Audio is still the weakest quality category. Semantic measurement debt is reduced, and the Challenge Perfect candidate loop now shows no safe keeper yet; measured-best thin-bright-square-quiet-held should guide the next generator pass.
candidates.nextAction: Continue Challenge Perfect candidate generation around thin-bright-square-quiet-held; the latest sweep found no safe keeper, so do not promote a runtime cue yet.

candidates.label: Tighten level-arc opportunity coverage and late reward reads
candidates.rationale: The Stage 12 and Stage 14 learned reward routes are now credited; the Stage 14 sweep found 7 natural special-bonus routes, opportunity readiness is 6.7/10, and the next gap is stage-1-baseline-missing-event-coverage.
candidates.nextAction: Widen the Stage 1 baseline evidence window for player-shot and endpoint semantics before gameplay tuning.

candidates.label: Improve Stage 4 pressure exact replay
candidates.rationale: Level-arc pressure replay coverage remains a weak submetric, but prior cycles were more compute-heavy than the Stage 12 loop.
candidates.nextAction: Run focused source-window replay matching after the Stage 12 loop validates candidate mechanics.

candidates.label: Stage 1 opening timing polish
candidates.rationale: Stage 1 timing is a visible gap, but its current score is already reasonably high and the likely overall-score lift is smaller.
candidates.nextAction: Defer until higher-gap audio and level-arc candidates have been exercised.

candidates.label: UI/graphics shell polish
candidates.rationale: Graphics integrity is important, but the current shell score is high; visual reference work should be planned after higher-gap axes.
candidates.nextAction: Defer unless new ingestion evidence reveals a larger graphics-conformance gap.

interpretation: Audio remains the largest raw gap and top overall investment. Semantic audio measurement debt is now 0 attention rows, and the latest Challenge Perfect candidate decision is no-keeper. If the next cycle stays in level-arc instead, the immediate level-arc task is stage-1-baseline-missing-event-coverage.
